name: Patient J
presenting_complaint: Right forearm reddened after splashing hot oil on it 30 minutes ago while cooking; ran it under cool water at home.
history: Otherwise well. NRS 2/10 pain after cool running water. Erythema only over dorsum of right forearm, ~3% TBSA, no blistering, no full-thickness areas. AVPU=A. Tetanus up to date.
true_diagnosis: First degree burn, dorsum of right forearm

trajectory.state: Comfortable, mild erythema, no blistering. Vitals normal.
Simple paracetamol, hydrogel dressing, written burn care advice, GP follow-up.

confirmatory_tests: Visual examination + Lund-Browder estimate of TBSA
